description: Controlled relationship vocabulary for the Domain Knowledge Base.
storage_policy: Store canonical direction only; do not materialize virtual inverse edges.
inference_policy: No automatic inference except explicitly approved relationship behavior.

relationship_types.description: The source is a more specific kind or subtype of the target.
relationship_types.usage_guidance: Use only for genuine subtype-to-supertype classification.
do_not_use_when: The source is merely a component or member of the target; use PART_OF.
relationship_types.example_statement: Illustrative only: Synthetic subtype IS_A Synthetic parent type.

relationship_types.description: The source provides a concrete implementation of the specification, standard, interface or abstract capability represented by the target.
relationship_types.usage_guidance: Use when a concrete artifact realizes a named specification, interface, standard or abstract capability.
do_not_use_when: The concepts are only similar or associated without an implementation claim.
relationship_types.example_statement: Illustrative only: Synthetic component IMPLEMENTS Synthetic interface.

relationship_types.description: The source is a constituent component or structural part of the target.
relationship_types.usage_guidance: Use for direct structural containment; v1.0 does not infer containment through intermediate parts.
do_not_use_when: The source is a subtype of the target; use IS_A.
The relationship is only functional use or dependency.
relationship_types.example_statement: Illustrative only: Synthetic component PART_OF Synthetic system.

relationship_types.description: The source directly uses the target while performing its function.
relationship_types.usage_guidance: Use for direct functional consumption without asserting mandatory operational dependency.
do_not_use_when: The target is only background context.
The source cannot operate without the target; consider DEPENDS_ON or REQUIRES.
relationship_types.example_statement: Illustrative only: Synthetic service USES Synthetic interface.

relationship_types.description: The source changes or enforces configuration settings on the target.
relationship_types.usage_guidance: Use when changing or enforcing settings is the specific asserted action.
do_not_use_when: The action is installation, updating, passive observation or generic management.
relationship_types.example_statement: Illustrative only: Synthetic policy controller CONFIGURES Synthetic endpoint.

relationship_types.description: The source makes a capability represented by the target possible or available.
relationship_types.usage_guidance: Use for a documented enabling mechanism that makes the target capability available.
do_not_use_when: The target is mandatory for the source; use REQUIRES.
The concepts are merely related.
relationship_types.example_statement: Illustrative only: Synthetic mechanism ENABLES Synthetic capability.

relationship_types.description: The source performs initialization of the target during startup, boot or device activation.
relationship_types.usage_guidance: Use for a documented startup, boot or activation initialization action.
do_not_use_when: The action is general management after initialization.
relationship_types.example_statement: Illustrative only: Synthetic startup component INITIALIZES Synthetic device.

relationship_types.description: The source administers, controls or governs the operational state of the target.
relationship_types.usage_guidance: Use for active administration, control or governance of operational state.
do_not_use_when: The source only observes the target; use MONITORS.
relationship_types.example_statement: Illustrative only: Synthetic controller MANAGES Synthetic endpoint.

relationship_types.description: The source observes or collects status, health, telemetry or events from the target.
relationship_types.usage_guidance: Use for passive or observational collection of telemetry, health, status or events.
do_not_use_when: The source changes, configures or governs the target; use CONFIGURES or MANAGES.
relationship_types.example_statement: Illustrative only: Synthetic observer MONITORS Synthetic endpoint.

relationship_types.description: The source provides a security protection function for the target.
relationship_types.usage_guidance: Use when a documented security mechanism protects a specific target.
do_not_use_when: The source is only generally associated with security or the target.
relationship_types.example_statement: Illustrative only: Synthetic control PROTECTS Synthetic resource.

relationship_types.description: The source delivers, applies or manages an update to the target.
relationship_types.usage_guidance: Use when update delivery, application or orchestration is the specific behavior.
do_not_use_when: Only generic management or configuration behavior is known.
relationship_types.example_statement: Illustrative only: Synthetic update service UPDATES Synthetic component.

relationship_types.description: The source operationally depends on the target, but the relationship is not necessarily a formal published requirement.
relationship_types.usage_guidance: Use for evidenced operational dependency when a formal mandatory requirement is not established.
do_not_use_when: The source merely uses the target without operational dependency.
Do not infer dependency transitively.
relationship_types.example_statement: Illustrative only: Synthetic service DEPENDS_ON Synthetic runtime.

relationship_types.description: The source is deployed or installed on the target endpoint, operating system or platform.
relationship_types.usage_guidance: Use for explicit deployment or installation location claims.
do_not_use_when: The source is a built-in structural component; use PART_OF unless installation is specifically relevant.
relationship_types.example_statement: Illustrative only: Synthetic agent INSTALLED_ON Synthetic endpoint.

relationship_types.description: The target is mandatory for the source under the specified scope and conditions.
relationship_types.usage_guidance: Use only when authoritative evidence establishes that the target is mandatory for the source.
do_not_use_when: The integration is optional, merely useful or only generally dependent.
relationship_types.example_statement: Illustrative only: Synthetic feature REQUIRES Synthetic prerequisite.

relationship_types.description: The source executes within or on the target operating system or execution platform.
relationship_types.usage_guidance: Use for execution-host claims, with platform or version conditions where applicable.
do_not_use_when: Only compatibility or installation is known; use the more precise predicate.
relationship_types.example_statement: Illustrative only: Synthetic application RUNS_ON Synthetic platform.

relationship_types.description: The source has been formally deprecated in favour of the target.
relationship_types.usage_guidance: Use only when an authoritative source formally deprecates the source in favour of the target.
do_not_use_when: No formal deprecation statement exists or the target is merely newer.
relationship_types.example_statement: Illustrative only: Synthetic legacy feature DEPRECATED_BY Synthetic successor.

relationship_types.description: The source supersedes the target as a successor technology or concept.
relationship_types.usage_guidance: Use for authoritative successor claims where the source supersedes the target.
do_not_use_when: The source is merely newer or coexists with the target without superseding it.
relationship_types.example_statement: Illustrative only: Synthetic successor REPLACES Synthetic predecessor.

relationship_types.description: The source can operate with the target under explicitly documented conditions.
relationship_types.usage_guidance: Use for explicitly documented operational compatibility with complete conditions and canonical direction.
do_not_use_when: Compatibility is inferred, unscoped or assumed symmetric.
relationship_types.example_statement: Illustrative only: Synthetic component COMPATIBLE_WITH Synthetic platform under stated test conditions.

relationship_types.description: The source is documented to conflict or operate incorrectly with the target under specified conditions.
relationship_types.usage_guidance: Use only for authoritative, condition-scoped conflict or malfunction documentation.
do_not_use_when: There is only suspicion, lack of support, or an undocumented incompatibility.
relationship_types.example_statement: Illustrative only: Synthetic component CONFLICTS_WITH Synthetic platform under stated test conditions.

relationship_types.description: The source officially supports operation, integration or use with the target under defined conditions.
relationship_types.usage_guidance: Use only for official support statements with explicit conditions and authoritative evidence.
do_not_use_when: Compatibility is inferred or operation is observed without an official support commitment.
relationship_types.example_statement: Illustrative only: Synthetic vendor product SUPPORTS Synthetic platform under stated conditions.
